description.to:
As a procedures-dashboard user, I want optimizer datasets and cycle-table modes to use Recent/Regression terminology and mode-specific columns, so that optimizer output is unambiguous and consistent across state and UI.

Feature: Procedure dashboard optimizer nomenclature and mode-specific cycles table

Scenario: Overall mode shows dual dataset deltas and costs
Given an optimizer procedure with baseline and cycle metrics
When I view the ProcedureTask in Overall mode
Then I see Recent and Regression dataset blocks
And each block shows AC1, delta, Accuracy, delta, and Cost/item
And precision/recall columns are not shown

Scenario: Recent mode shows only Recent quality metrics
Given an optimizer procedure with baseline and cycle metrics
When I switch the optimizer view to Recent
Then the Cycles table shows only the Recent dataset block
And it shows AC1, Accuracy, Precision, and Recall
And it does not show deltas or cost columns

Scenario: Regression mode shows only Regression quality metrics
Given an optimizer procedure with baseline and cycle metrics
When I switch the optimizer view to Regression
Then the Cycles table shows only the Regression dataset block
And it shows AC1, Accuracy, Precision, and Recall
And it does not show deltas or cost columns

Scenario: Optimizer state contract uses Recent/Regression keys
Given optimizer state is persisted for dashboard consumption
When a new cycle is stored
Then dataset keys are Recent/Regression (not Feedback/Accuracy)
And each cycle includes recent_cost_per_item and regression_cost_per_item
And dashboard parsing reads only the new keys without compatibility fallbacks
